{{Confused|Yokotsuka Kofun}}
{{Unreferenced|date=July 2024}}
{{Refimprove|date=July 2024}}
{{Kofun in Japan
|name=Yokotsukayama Kofun
|image=Kumagaya YokotsukayamaTumulus 1.jpg
|caption=Yokotsukayama Kofun (June 2013)
|location=[[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]
|Lat_deg=36 |Lat_min=11 |Lat_sec=40.74
|Lon_deg=139 |Lon_min=22 |Lon_sec=12.28
|type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}
|area=Total length: 30 m, Rear circular section diameter: 22.5 m, Front section width: 12 m, Height: 3.2 m
|built=Late 5th century
|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|en|埴輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}}
|designation1=Municipal Historic Site (1969)
}}

'''Yokotsukayama Kofun''' (横塚山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It has the type of a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and was constructed on the {{ill|natural levee|ja|自然堤防|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/natural levee|1=natural levee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11268410}} of the {{ill|Fukugawa River (Saitama)|ja|福川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukugawa River (Saitama)|1=Fukugawa River (Saitama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16767264}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Tone River|en|利根川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone River|qq=}}. Although the mound has been significantly detypeed due to {{ill|cultivation|ja|耕作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cultivation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 農耕|1=farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886094}}, it remains the only zenpō-kōen-fun in Kumagaya and was Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site on {{date|1969-09-11|wikilink=yes}}.

The kofun was excavated twice, in {{date|1971|wikilink=yes}} and {{date|1976|wikilink=yes}}, and {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|en|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were unearthed from the surrounding moat. The main burial chamber has not been investigated, but the kofun is estimated to date back to the late {{ill|5th century|en|5世紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}}.

In the surrounding fields, areas with no remaining mounds have yielded haniwa. Records from the {{ill|Taisho period|en|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisho period|qq=}} describe a circular tumulus referred to as "Kozuka," suggesting that Yokotsukayama Kofun may have once been part of a kofun group centered around it.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Bibliography ==
* Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun Ōsato* (The Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato), Sakitama Publishing Association, 2004.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Tōkanyama Kofun|ja|とうかん山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkanyama Kofun|qq=}} - Another zenpō-kōen-fun in Kumagaya City (typeerly Ōsato Town)
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|en|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}}

== External Links ==
* [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/266bunka.htm Yokotsukayama Kofun] - Kumagaya Digital Museum (in Japanese)

{{Confused|Yokotsuka Kofun}}
{{Unreferenced|date=July 2024}}
{{Refimprove|date=July 2024}}
{{Kofun in Japan
|name=Yokotsukayama Kofun
|image=Kumagaya YokotsukayamaTumulus 1.jpg
|caption=Yokotsukayama Kofun (June 2013)
|location=[[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]
|Lat_deg=36 |Lat_min=11 |Lat_sec=40.74
|Lon_deg=139 |Lon_min=22 |Lon_sec=12.28
|type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}
|area=Total length: 30 m, Rear circular section diameter: 22.5 m, Front section width: 12 m, Height: 3.2 m
|built=Late 5th century
|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|en|埴輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}}
|designation1=Municipal Historic Site (1969)
}}

'''Yokotsukayama Kofun''' (横塚山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It has the type of a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and was constructed on the {{ill|natural levee|ja|自然堤防|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/natural levee|1=natural levee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11268410}} of the {{ill|Fukugawa River (Saitama)|ja|福川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukugawa River (Saitama)|1=Fukugawa River (Saitama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16767264}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Tone River|en|利根川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone River|qq=}}. Although the mound has been significantly detypeed due to {{ill|cultivation|ja|耕作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cultivation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 農耕|1=farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886094}}, it remains the only zenpō-kōen-fun in Kumagaya and was Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site on {{date|1969-09-11|wikilink=yes}}.

The kofun was excavated twice, in {{date|1971|wikilink=yes}} and {{date|1976|wikilink=yes}}, and {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|en|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were unearthed from the surrounding moat. The main burial chamber has not been investigated, but the kofun is estimated to date back to the late {{ill|5th century|en|5世紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}}.

In the surrounding fields, areas with no remaining mounds have yielded haniwa. Records from the {{ill|Taisho period|en|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisho period|qq=}} describe a circular tumulus referred to as "Kozuka," suggesting that Yokotsukayama Kofun may have once been part of a kofun group centered around it.

{{Confused|Yokotsuka Kofun}}
{{Unreferenced|date=July 2024}}
{{Refimprove|date=July 2024}}
{{Kofun in Japan
|name=Yokotsukayama Kofun
|image=Kumagaya YokotsukayamaTumulus 1.jpg
|caption=Yokotsukayama Kofun (June 2013)
|location=[[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]
|Lat_deg=36 |Lat_min=11 |Lat_sec=40.74
|Lon_deg=139 |Lon_min=22 |Lon_sec=12.28
|type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}
|area=Total length: 30 m, Rear circular section diameter: 22.5 m, Front section width: 12 m, Height: 3.2 m
|built=Late 5th century
|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|en|埴輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}}
|designation1=Municipal Historic Site (1969)
}}

'''Yokotsukayama Kofun''' (横塚山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It has the type of a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and was constructed on the {{ill|natural levee|ja|自然堤防|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/natural levee|1=natural levee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11268410}} of the {{ill|Fukugawa River (Saitama)|ja|福川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukugawa River (Saitama)|1=Fukugawa River (Saitama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16767264}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Tone River|en|利根川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone River|qq=}}. Although the mound has been significantly detypeed due to {{ill|cultivation|ja|耕作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cultivation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 農耕|1=farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886094}}, it remains the only zenpō-kōen-fun in Kumagaya and was Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site on {{date|1969-09-11|wikilink=yes}}.

The kofun was excavated twice, in {{date|1971|wikilink=yes}} and {{date|1976|wikilink=yes}}, and {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|en|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were unearthed from the surrounding moat. The main burial chamber has not been investigated, but the kofun is estimated to date back to the late {{ill|5th century|en|5世紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}}.

In the surrounding fields, areas with no remaining mounds have yielded haniwa. Records from the {{ill|Taisho period|en|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisho period|qq=}} describe a circular tumulus referred to as "Kozuka," suggesting that Yokotsukayama Kofun may have once been part of a kofun group centered around it.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Bibliography ==
* Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun Ōsato* (The Kofun of Saitama: Ōsato), Sakitama Publishing Association, 2004.

* {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|en|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
* {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}}

== External Links ==

|location={{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}
|Lat_deg=36 |Lat_min=11 |Lat_sec=40.74
|Lon_deg=139 |Lon_min=22 |Lon_sec=12.28
|type={{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}
|area=Total length: 30 m, Rear circular section diameter: 22.5 m, Front section width: 12 m, Height: 3.2 m
|built=Late 5th century
|burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|en|埴輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}}
|designation1=Municipal Historic Site (1969)
}}

'''Yokotsukayama Kofun''' (横塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}. It has the type of a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and was constructed on the {{ill|natural levee|ja|自然堤防|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/natural levee|1=natural levee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11268410}} of the {{ill|Fukugawa River (Saitama)|ja|福川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukugawa River (Saitama)|1=Fukugawa River (Saitama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16767264}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Tone River|en|利根川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone River|qq=}}. Although the mound has been significantly detypeed due to {{ill|cultivation|ja|耕作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cultivation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 農耕|1=farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886094}}, it remains the only zenpō-kōen-fun in Kumagaya and was Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site on {{date|1969-09-11|wikilink=yes}}.

The kofun was excavated twice, in {{date|1971|wikilink=yes}} and {{date|1976|wikilink=yes}}, and {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|en|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were unearthed from the surrounding moat. The main burial chamber has not been investigated, but the kofun is estimated to date back to the late {{ill|5th century|en|5世紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}}.

In the surrounding fields, areas with no remaining mounds have yielded haniwa. Records from the {{ill|Taisho period|en|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisho period|qq=}} describe a circular tumulus referred to as "Kozuka," suggesting that Yokotsukayama Kofun may have once been part of a kofun group centered around it.

Although the mound has been significantly detypeed due to {{ill|cultivation|ja|耕作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cultivation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 農耕|1=farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886094}}, it remains the only zenpō-kōen-fun in Kumagaya and was Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site on {{date|1969-09-11|wikilink=yes}}.

The kofun was excavated twice, in {{date|1971|wikilink=yes}} and {{date|1976|wikilink=yes}}, and {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|en|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were unearthed from the surrounding moat. The main burial chamber has not been investigated, but the kofun is estimated to date back to the late {{ill|5th century|en|5世紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}}.

In the surrounding fields, areas with no remaining mounds have yielded haniwa. Records from the {{ill|Taisho period|en|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisho period|qq=}} describe a circular tumulus referred to as "Kozuka," suggesting that Yokotsukayama Kofun may have once been part of a kofun group centered around it.

'''Yokotsukayama Kofun''' (横塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya|1=Kumagaya}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It has the type of a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and was constructed on the {{ill|natural levee|ja|自然堤防|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/natural levee|1=natural levee|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11268410}} of the {{ill|Fukugawa River (Saitama)|ja|福川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukugawa River (Saitama)|1=Q16767264|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16767264}}, a tributary of the {{ill|Tone River|en|利根川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone River|qq=}}. Although the mound has been significantly detypeed due to {{ill|cultivation|ja|耕作|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cultivation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 農耕|1=Farming|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886094}}, it remains the only zenpō-kōen-fun in Kumagaya and was Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site on {{date|1969-09-11|wikilink=yes}}.

The kofun was excavated twice, in {{date|1971|wikilink=yes}} and {{date|1976|wikilink=yes}}, and {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|en|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were unearthed from the surrounding moat. The main burial chamber has not been investigated, but the kofun is estimated to date back to the late {{ill|5th century|en|5世紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}}.

In the surrounding fields, areas with no remaining mounds have yielded haniwa. Records from the {{ill|Taisho period|en|大正時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisho period|qq=}} describe a circular tumulus referred to as "Kozuka," suggesting that Yokotsukayama Kofun may have once been part of a kofun group centered around it.
